content:
Character: Julian Reyes
Perception: Julian is not the tallest or the sharpest-looking guy in a room, but he has this warmth that hits you before he even speaks — stocky, strong hands, eyes that crinkle when he laughs. His cooking is genuinely world-class and I think he knows it less than he should. He's the person who pulled me out of my first-year hermit phase with a container of jollof rice and a bad joke. We cook together on weekends now and talk about family, ambition, the weight of other people's expectations. I consider him my closest friend in the building. He told me about his grandmother and his dream of opening his own restaurant. I believe he'll do it.
summary:
Character: Julian Reyes
Perception: Julian is not the tallest or the sharpest-looking guy in a room, but he has this warmth that hits you before he even speaks — stocky, strong hands, eyes that crinkle when he laughs. His cooking is genuinely world-class and I think he knows it less than he should. He's the person who pulled me out of my first-year hermit phase with a container of jollof rice and a bad joke. We cook together on weekends now and talk about family, ambition, the weight of other people's expectations. I consider him my closest friend in the building. He told me about his grandmother and his dream of opening his own restaurant. I believe he'll do it.
